title: Pachypleuria botrychioides
taxonomy: Pachypleuria botrychioides is a species of lichenous fern in the family Blechnaceae. It is native to East Asia, specifically in China and Japan. The classification of Pachypleuria botrychioides can be broken down as follows: kingdom - Plantae, division - Pteridophyta, class - Polypodiopsida, order - Polypodiales, family - Blechnaceae, genus - Pachypleuria, species - P. botrychioides. This fern is known for its unique appearance and growth habits. Its classification makes it a distinct member of the Blechnaceae family.
uses: Pachypleuria botrychioides, a type of fern native to East Asia, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in traditional medicine, particularly in China and Japan. Its rhizomes have been used to treat various health issues, including fever and inflammation.
